statement.p: One of the tires on your car looks a little flat. You measure its air pressure and are alarmed to see it so low at {21\ {\rm psi}}\text{.} You have a portable device that can pump air into the tire increasing the pressure at a rate of {2.4\ {\textstyle\frac{\rm\mathstrut psi}{\rm\mathstrut min}}}\text{.} How long will it take to fill the tire to the manuals recommended pressure of {34\ {\rm psi}}\text{?}

solution.p: This is a rate scenario. The air pressure starts out at 21\text{,} and increases with a rate of 2.4\text{.} Eventually the pressure reaches 34\text{.} So the equation we set up is:
where t is the number of minutes the pump runs. Now we follow the standard steps to solve this equation.
So it will take {5.41667\ {\rm min}} for the pump to fill the tire.
